title: McCarthy Tétrault Bolsters its National Labour & Employment Practice with New Partner Stephanie Henry
content:
## McCarthy Tétrault Bolsters its National Labour & Employment Practice with New Partner Stephanie Henry
We are proud to announce that Stephanie Henry has joined McCarthy Tétrault’s Calgary office as a partner in the Employment & Labour Law Group. Well-known in the Alberta market and ranked by Chambers and Partners, Stephanie will support our clients with their complex employment and union matters in Canada and beyond.
Stephanie helps employers manage all aspects of the employment relationship, including hiring and employment agreements, termination issues, human rights and accommodation issues, incentive compensation issues, disability management, workplace investigations and terminations. She also handles all dispute resolution matters, including wrongful dismissal litigation, human rights complaints, and labour arbitration matters.
Stephanie's expertise, from drafting employment agreements to representing employers in complex labour arbitrations, is a welcome addition to our top-tier Labour & Employment Group, which help clients navigate an ever-changing business landscape, mitigate risks, and address both their strategic goals and day-to-day legal challenges.
“Stephanie’s stellar reputation and expertise are the perfect complement to our market-leading team in Alberta,” said Tim Lawson , National Practice Group Leader of McCarthy Tetrault’s Labour & Employment Group. “Our clients are looking for full-service capability in employment and labour matters, and Stephanie is known for her talent in handling high-stakes, high-profile matters.”
Ranked in Chambers Canada as “Up and Coming” for Employment and Labour – Alberta, Stephanie advises some of the major players in the energy industry, as well as national telecommunications companies and public-sector entities. Her arrival adds to McCarthy Tétrault’s bench strength in serving Tier 1 clients headquartered in both Canada and the United States.
She also has extensive transactional experience, and her seasoned labour and employment skills will benefit the firm’s market-leading counsel advising on mergers and acquisitions, corporate financings, tax matters and commercial litigation.
“I’m thrilled to be joining the Labour & Employment Law Group at McCarthy Tétrault in Calgary,” Stephanie said. "The firm's commitment to excellence and its strong presence in the Alberta market make this an exciting opportunity. Together with the firm’s national presence and my expertise, I can help our clients devise novel strategies to efficiently achieve their business goals. I look forward to collaborating with my colleagues and contributing to the firm's mission in delivering innovative and strategic solutions for our clients.”
Ranked Band 1 by Chambers Canada and recognized as Canada’s top employment and labour law group in a full-service firm by Canadian HR Reporter, McCarthy Tétrault’s Labour & Employment Group can be relied on to provide practical, effective advice and human resource strategies. Canadian and international clients benefit from the national scope of our team and its extensive experience in all workplace issues, such as employment and human rights, unionization and collective bargaining pensions and benefits, occupational health and safety and workplace safety and insurance.
About McCarthy Tétrault
McCarthy Tétrault LLP provides a broad range of legal services, providing strategic and industry-focused advice and solutions for Canadian and international interests. The Firm has substantial presence in Canada’s major commercial centres as well as in New York and London.
Built on an integrated approach to the practice of law and delivery of innovative client services, the Firm brings its legal talent, industry insight and practice experience to help clients achieve the results that are important to them.
For further information, or to request an interview with a McCarthy Tétrault lawyer, please contact:
- Stephanie Henry Partner People.Offices.Singular Calgary
